vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter base (console shift) or slot on steering column (column shift)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle uses a COLUMN-MOUNTED shifter — the shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN, not the floor console.
Locate the slot on the steering column — Cap at side of console shifter base (console shift) or slot on steering column (column shift).
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot on the column.
Press firmly while pressing the brake pedal.
Move the column shifter to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and move column shifter to N normally.
vehicles.notes: 4WD: transfer case MUST be in 2H before towing on paved road.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric push-button (Autotrac) on most; manual floor lever on some older bench-seat trims
positions: 2WD (2H) — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD — NOT safe for towing
4WD Hi (4H) — part-time 4WD high — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo (4L) — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH (electric TC) or stop completely (manual lever).
ELECTRIC (push-button on dash/console): Press the 2WD button. Wait for the 2WD indicator lamp to illuminate.
MANUAL LEVER: Stop the vehicle completely. Move the floor-mounted TC lever firmly to the 2H position.
Confirm the 2WD indicator lamp is ON on the instrument cluster.
Verify NO 4WD indicator lights are illuminated before lifting rear wheels.
NEVER tow in 4H, 4WD Auto, or 4L on pavement — will damage axles and transfer case.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

knownIssues.title: Transfer Case in 4H/4L Will Destroy Driveline
knownIssues.detail: Towing a 4WD Silverado with the transfer case in 4H or 4L on dry pavement — even for short distances — will cause severe transfer case and differential damage. Always confirm 2WD indicator is illuminated before lifting. This is the single most common towing damage claim on these trucks.

battery.warnings: Confirm TC in 2WD before wheel lift. Most common towing damage on this platform is from towing in 4H.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — trim panel near selector, remove to expose slot
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — trim panel near selector, remove to expose slot.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.warn: Silverado EV: FLATBED ONLY. 4WD: transfer case.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric rotary dial on center console (Autotrac)
positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD — NOT safe for towing
4WD Hi — part-time 4WD — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH.
Turn the rotary transfer case dial on the center console to 2WD.
Wait for the 2WD indicator to illuminate on the instrument cluster.
Verify all 4WD indicator lights are OFF before lifting rear wheels.
Silverado EV (2024+): FLATBED ONLY — no TC, electric drivetrain.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.slot: Cap at console shifter base or electronic selector slot depending on year
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cap at console shifter base or electronic selector slot depending on year.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood — may have dual batteries on diesel trims.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.notes: Duramax diesel: same procedure. Heavy-duty — use heavy wheel blocks.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first. Heavy-duty — use rated blocks. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

tc.type: Electric push-button (newer) or manual floor lever (older) — NV246 or NV261 transfer case
positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4H — part-time 4WD high range — NOT safe for dry-road towing
N — neutral (some models) — used for flatbed loading only
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: ELECTRIC: Slow under 3 MPH. Press 2H button. Confirm 2H indicator illuminates.
MANUAL: Stop the vehicle completely. Move TC floor lever firmly to 2H.
Confirm 2H indicator before lifting rear wheels.
Heavy-duty truck — use properly rated wheel blocks, chains, and straps.
Do NOT tow in 4H or 4L on pavement.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: MANUAL TRANSFER CASE (floor lever): Vehicle must be STOPPED to shift. If lever is stiff, rock vehicle slightly in neutral while applying firm pressure.
ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
DIESEL: Same shift-lock and tow procedures as gasoline variant. Heavier curb weight — account for extra weight in equipment ratings.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

battery.location: Under hood — may have dual batteries on diesel trims
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: jump to positive (+) terminal on either battery. Ground (−) to engine block or frame.
battery.warnings: Diesel trims may have two 12V batteries in parallel. Jump to either positive terminal. Heavy vehicle — verify equipment ratings.

vehicles.slot: Cap at side of console shifter base
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap at side of console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap at side of console shifter base.
vehicles.warn: 4WD: transfer case. Large SUV — block all wheels.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD/2WD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. 4WD: transfer case to 2H first, then lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.

positions: 2WD — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4WD Auto — automatic AWD — NOT safe for towing
4WD Hi — part-time 4WD — NOT safe for dry-road towing
4WD Lo — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow to under 3 MPH.
Press the 2WD button on the transfer case control panel.
Wait for the 2WD indicator lamp to illuminate.
Verify all 4WD lights are OFF before lifting rear wheels.
Large SUV — use heavy wheel blocks before releasing Park.
IF BATTERY IS DEAD: Jump battery first to power electronic TC, shift to 2WD, then disconnect. If unable to shift, FLATBED ONLY.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.
DEAD BATTERY FALLBACK: If TC cannot be shifted to 2WD, USE FLATBED. Do NOT tow with TC in 4WD/4H/4L — will destroy transfer case and driveline.
HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.
4WD DOLLY FALLBACK: If transfer case CANNOT be shifted to 2WD (dead battery, mechanical failure), either use flatbed OR wheel-lift with dollies on the opposite axle.

vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

positions: 2H — rear wheels only — REQUIRED for all road towing
4H — part-time 4WD high range
4L — low range — NEVER tow in this position
steps: Slow under 3 MPH.
Select 2H on transfer case control.
Confirm indicator before lifting rear wheels.

towNotes: ELECTRONIC TRANSFER CASE: Requires 12V power to shift. If battery is dead, jump the battery FIRST, then shift TC to 2WD before disconnecting.

vehicles.slot: Cap near center tunnel shifter — remove to access slot
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near center tunnel shifter — remove to access slot.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood (in front storage area).
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near center tunnel shifter — remove to access slot.
vehicles.notes: Manual: clutch to N. Z06: same.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — extremely low clearance, no wheel lift. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — in the front storage compartment

towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — in the front storage compartment

vehicles.slot: Removable panel near electronic selector on console — remove to expose slot
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Removable panel near electronic selector on console — remove to expose slot.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Open the front hood — the 12V auxiliary battery is in the frunk (front storage area).  Connect jump pack to the 12V terminals.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.notes: Battery is in the REAR TRUNK (right rear under carpet). Z06, Grand Sport, ZR1: same.
vehicles.warn: FLATBED ONLY. Extremely low clearance. Load from REAR only.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — extremely low clearance, load from rear only
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

knownIssues.title: Battery in Frunk — Not Engine Bay
knownIssues.detail: The C7 Corvette has a front trunk (frunk) where the battery is located, NOT in the engine bay. Operators who don't know this will waste significant time looking for jump terminals. Open the hood and look in the front storage compartment.

knownIssues.title: Extremely Low Ground Clearance
knownIssues.detail: Standard ground clearance is approximately 4.5 inches. Lowered or Z51 variants are even lower. Any approach angle to a flatbed must be carefully managed with ramps or approach plates. Load from rear only — never winch from front over a standard ramp angle.

knownIssues.title: Carbon Ceramic Brakes — No Metal-to-Metal Contact
knownIssues.detail: Many C7 Z06 and Grand Sport trims have carbon ceramic brake rotors. These are extremely expensive ($15,000+). Never use metal wheel chocks directly against the rotors. Use rubber-padded chocks only.

battery.location: FRUNK (front trunk) — NOT in the engine bay. Open hood and look in front storage compartment.
battery.jumpTerminals: Inside the frunk — standard red (+) / black (−) terminals on battery
battery.warnings: Many operators waste time looking for the battery in the engine bay. It is NOT there. Open the hood — the frunk IS the storage area. Very low clearance — flatbed only.

vehicles.slot: Cover near electronic selector on center console — remove to expose slot
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cover near electronic selector on center console — remove to expose slot.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Behind passenger seat OR in front storage — depends on trim. NOT in engine bay (mid-engine vehicle)..
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.notes: C8 is MID-ENGINE — battery location differs from all previous Corvettes.
vehicles.warn: FLATBED ONLY for all C8. Extremely low clearance. Do not winch from front or rear.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — mid-engine, extremely low clearance. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle.
towNotes: LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
DUAL-CLUTCH TRANSMISSION (DCT): Power is strongly preferred to shift to neutral. DCT without power may not release — use flatbed if shift override fails.

knownIssues.title: Mid-Engine — Battery Behind Passenger Seat
knownIssues.detail: Unlike all previous Corvettes, the C8 is mid-engine. The 12V battery is located behind the passenger seat or in the front storage area — NOT in the engine bay. There is no frunk battery on most C8 trims.

knownIssues.title: Absolutely No Winching From Front or Rear Bumper
knownIssues.detail: The C8 front and rear fascias have virtually zero structural tolerance for winching. Damage to the carbon fiber body panels is extremely costly. Use only designated tow hooks — front tow hook is accessed through the front fascia opening on the driver side.

knownIssues.title: Extremely Low Ground Clearance
knownIssues.detail: Ground clearance is approximately 4.5 inches stock. Z06 and ZR1 trims may be lower. Use approach ramps or extended ramps for flatbed loading. Approach angle is critical.

knownIssues.title: E-Ray Hybrid AWD
knownIssues.detail: C8 E-Ray (2024+) is a hybrid AWD. FLATBED ONLY for E-Ray. 12V terminals only — never access the HV front axle motor system.

battery.location: Behind passenger seat OR in front storage — depends on trim. NOT in engine bay (mid-engine vehicle).
battery.jumpTerminals: Behind passenger seat: pull seat forward panel to access. OR front storage compartment on some trims.
battery.warnings: Mid-engine — no conventional engine bay battery. E-Ray hybrid: 12V only, never touch HV front motor. Flatbed only.

vehicles.slot: Cap near shifter base (older) or slot near electronic selector (2016+)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cap near shifter base (older) or slot near electronic selector (2016+).
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

vehicles.slot: Cap near selector base on console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near selector base on console.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near selector base on console.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air, or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Cap near console shifter base
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Cap near console shifter base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side near firewall.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Cap near console shifter base.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.location: Under hood, driver side near firewall

vehicles.slot: Slot near console shifter base — trim cover may need removal first
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Slot near console shifter base — trim cover may need removal first.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.notes: ZL1, 1LE: same. Manual: clutch to N.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near shifter on console
shift: Turn the Chevrolet Volt to ON/Ready mode.
Shift lock slot: Electronic selector — slot near shifter on console.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move selector to N.
FLATBED ONLY — do not tow with driven wheels on the ground.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood (12V auxiliary battery). 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY.
vehicles.notes: PHEV. HV battery runs down floor tunnel.
vehicles.warn: FLATBED ONLY. Never tow with driven wheels on ground.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — electric drivetrain
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

knownIssues.title: FLATBED ONLY — PHEV Drivetrain
knownIssues.detail: The Volt's electric drive system means the driven wheels CANNOT rotate freely. Towing with driven wheels on the ground will damage the electric motor and inverter. Flatbed is the only acceptable method.

knownIssues.title: Never Access HV Battery
knownIssues.detail: The HV battery runs the full length of the floor tunnel. The 12V auxiliary battery under the hood is the only battery to be used for jumping. The orange high-voltage cables and connectors are not to be touched under any circumstances.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — 12V auxiliary terminals only. Red (+) near firewall, Black (−) ground bolt.
battery.warnings: NEVER touch the HV floor battery (orange cables). 12V auxiliary under hood ONLY.

vehicles.slot: Column shifter: slot on steering column. Console shift: cap at shifter base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle uses a COLUMN-MOUNTED shifter — the shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN, not the floor console.
Locate the slot on the steering column — Column shifter: slot on steering column. Console shift: cap at shifter base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot on the column.
Press firmly while pressing the brake pedal.
Move the column shifter to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and move column shifter to N normally.
vehicles.warn: Large van — use heavy wheel blocks before releasing Park.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: HEAVY VEHICLE: Use properly rated wheel blocks, straps, and chains. Verify your equipment weight rating before attempting to load or lift.

vehicles.slot: Cap near shifter base or electronic selector slot depending on year
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Cap near shifter base or electronic selector slot depending on year.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
CVT EQUIPPED: Do NOT exceed 35 mph or 50 miles with drive wheels on ground. Flatbed strongly preferred for CVTs.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot near base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — slot near base.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front — front (drive) wheels in the air. AWD: flatbed only. Blazer EV: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front (front/drive wheels in the air) freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
